question_text: In 1494,...................published a treatise on the double-entry system of bookkeeping.

option_b: Members of the Executives, Advisers and Senior Civil Servants,

question_text: It was stipulated in the Audit Ordinance Act of 1956 that the Accountant-General must render the following reports but one to the Auditor-General for audit purposes.
option_b: An abstract of receipts and payments.
option_c: A Statement of Assets and Liabilities
option_d: A detailed Sttatement of Revenue and Expenditure.

question_text: The qualitative characteristics of financial information of public sector entities are both.................

option_a: Consultants, and other members of professional bodies
option_d: Oversight bodies, Donor Agencies and general public.

question_text: The financial information of public sector entities have the following qualities except.................
A statement of cash flow has the following but one of the activities.
Users of government financial information can be both................and................
A Revenue Collector must periodically present his cash book and receipt books for examination by.............
Arrears of Revenue are revenues collectable during a given financial year but which remains uncollected.................. after the close of that financial year.
Remittance of tax revenue must be made to the Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) not later than..............after deduction.

Internally Generated Revenues (IGR) collected by a Ministry/Extra Ministerial Office must be remitted to the Consolidated Revenue Fund (CRF) on or before.........of the subsequent month after collection.
...............is issued before the passage of the Appropriation Act to keep government services running.
.............. authorises the Accountant-General to issue funds to pay for the personal emoluments and other services provided in the Annual Estimates.

The Provisional General Warrant authorises the withdrawal of money from the CRF as may be necessary for carrying on the services of government for a period not exceeding................. and at a level not exceeding the level of those services prevailing in the previous financial year.
.............. may be issued in very exceptional cases, where virement is not possible and where an application for additional provision reveals such a degree of urgency.
............ authorises the Accountant- General to issue funds to pay for the additional personal emoluments and other services which must have been approved in Supplementary Estimates.
..........is issued when, as a result of circumstances which could not have been foreseen when the Annual Estimates were being compiled.
.................. entails receipts and payments for services that are not specified in the approved Annual Budget estimates.
The followings are the conditions for virement of funds except:
option_a: The amount to be vired, should not exceed any limit prescribed by the National Assembly
option_b: Virement should not be used to create a new sub-head or re-introduce items disallowed by the National Assembly.
option_c: Virement must not apply to queries raised by the Auditor General.
option_d: Virement must not be used to pad annual estimates.

question_text: ........... is issued where necessary, additional expenditure over and above that included in the General Warrant or in a Supplementary General Warrant,
...............will be issued where the Capital Expenditure Estimate has not come into operation at the commencement of the financial year.
................ is granted for a particular purpose and it must be retired in full when the purpose has been achieved.
...................... authorises the Accountant General to issue funds for expenditure on capital projects, as specified in the Annual Estimates
...................... authorises the Accountant General to issue funds to incur expenditure on supplementary capital projects, as contained in the Annual Supplementary Capital Estimates.

...................... authorises the Accountant General to issue funds and the Officers Controlling Expenditure to incur expenditure in certain 'circumstances on capital projects beyond the amounts provided for the year concerned in the Annual or Supplementary Capital Estimates.

............. is authorised by the Federal Executive Council (FEC) when an application for Supplementary provision reveals a degree of urgency which makes it essential that funds be immediately made available to meet expenditure which cannot be provided for. by virement or without serious injury to the public interest, be postponed until adequate provision-can be made by the National Assembly.
....................... Authorizes the reallocation of capital funds from one sub-head to another within the same budget vote where there are savings.
.............. are replenished from time to time during a financial year by the submission of paid vouchers to Sub- Accounting Officers for reimbursement.
The following are conditions for capital development fund virement warrant except:
option_a: Fund re-allocation must be only within the same Head of Expenditure in the Capital Estimates.
option_b: The re-allocation may be part of the responses to Audit Query from the Auditor-General.
option_c: The re-allocation must not exceed the limits the Head of estimates approved by the National Assembly.
option_d: The re-allocation must not be intended to give rise to a new project.

Within..............of the end of every financial year, each self-accounting Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office is expected to a statutory Return on imprest received and retired to the Office of the Accountant General of the Federation.

Financial Authorities could be in form of any of the following except:

question_text: ...........is the Head of the Federal Government Accounting Services and Treasury
option_b: Director - Funds Management of the Federal Government
option_d: Finance Minister and Co-ordinating Minister of the Economy.

question_text: The Officer who is constitutionally responsible for the audit and report on the public accounts of the Federation is..............
option_d: Finance Minister and Co-ordinating Minister of the Economy.

question_text: ..............is responsible for providing adequate accounting systems and controls in the Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Departments and Agencies (MDAs) of Government.
option_b: Finance Minister and Co-ordinating Minister of the Economy.
option_c: Director - Funds Management of the Federal Government

question_text: The Auditor-General performs the following statutory functions except:
The Officer who is in full control of, and is responsible for human, material and financial resources which are critical inputs in the management of an organization.

................ is an officer who is entrusted with the receipt, custody and disbursement of public money and who is required to keep one of the recognised Cash books together with such other books of accounts.
The term "Sub-Accounting Officer" is applicable to the following officers except:
option_a: Director, Finance & Accounts or Head of Accounts
option_c: Federal Pay Officer, Police Pay Officer, Customs Area Pay Officer and Pensions Pay Officer.
option_d: Accountant General of the sub-national Government.

question_text: ................. is an officer, other than a Sub- Accounting officer, who is entrusted with an official receipt, licence or ticket booklet for the regular collection of some particular forms of revenue and who is required to keep a cash book.

A.............is one in· which the responsibility for the maintenance of the detailed record of expenditure and revenue and the Below-the-line payments and receipts ofthe ministry/extra-ministerial office and other arms of government or unit is delegated to the Accounting Officer.
option_c: Finance Attache in the Nigerian Embassy.

question_text: ..................is a managerial control which functions by measuring and evaluating the effectiveness of lnternal Control system in an organisation.
Checked and passed vouchers received in the........................ must be promptly dealt with and, under no circumstance, must a voucher be held in that Unit for more than forty-eight (48) hours.

An officer will be precluded from carrying out the duties of internal audit staff if he has previously been engaged on..................... within the ministry, extra ministerial office and other arms of government.
The ranks of Internal Audit staff who are empowered to make use of audit stamps, must not be below............. in Abuja and..........outside Abuja.
............... is not transferable from one officer to another.
An..................is held after the close of business on the last business day of each financial year to examine the cash, bank balances and stamps in an MDA.
option_b: Annual Board of Agencies and Parastatals meeting

question_text: It is the responsibility of the Director, Treasury Inspectorate Department of the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation (OAGF) to convene.............
option_b: Annual Board of Agencies and Parastatals meeting

question_text: Members of the Board of Survey are........in number.

option_b: New, fairly new, and old Stores

question_text: .......... or...............must not be used in place of local purchase order or job order for the procurement of stores locally.
A Board of Enquiry may not be necessary due to the following except:
option_b: if it is an isolated incident.
option_c: if the identity of the officer responsible is indisputable.

question_text: The following are conditions for setting up a Board of Inquiry except:
option_b: if the identity of the officer responsible is indisputable.
option_c: if several officers are involved, and the loss took place over a period of time; and

question_text: Board of Survey (BoS) should consist of at least...............members while the Chairman of the BoS should not be below Grade Level..................

A query on an unauthorised variation of contract(s) and procurement shall be answered within..........of issue.

How many chapters are in the Financial Regulations?
Who endorsed the Preface of the 2009 Financial Regulations.
option_a: Dr. (Mrs.) Okonjo Iweala, Former Finance Minister of Nigeria.
option_d: Dr. Mansur Muhtar, Former Finance Minister of Nigeria.

question_text: Open Treasury Portal of the Federal Government of Nigeris is aimed at......................
option_a: ensuring transparency and accountability to the public as well as securing citizens' trust.
option_b: Engendering government openness in allowing citizens to elect their preferred candidates.
option_c: promoting people's access to intrerest free loans and grants.
option_d: enhancing transparency in the distribution of relief materials to victims of insecurity in the country.

option_b: Use of a proper treasury form
